global.clean_code_guide: Read .abtree/trees/implement/clean-code.md from the repo root for the project's clean-code rules and the 'Done means' / 'When to stop and ask' checklists.

steps.instruction: Find the spec in specs/ matching $LOCAL.change_request. Read its frontmatter and check the reviewed_by field. - If no spec exists: report that a spec must be created first via /abt refine. Return failure. - If reviewed_by is empty: report the spec needs codeowner approval. Return failure. - Otherwise: store the full spec content at $LOCAL.spec_content.
Analyze $LOCAL.spec_content. Identify the files to modify and the logic required. Draft a focused implementation plan and store it at $LOCAL.plan.

Read $GLOBAL.clean_code_guide and follow it while implementing $LOCAL.plan. Apply the rules to every file you write, and treat the guide's "Done means" and "When to stop and ask" sections as part of this step's success criteria.
